goal: Optimize the ledger table so horizontal scrolling no longer causes users to lose row identity while working through downstream procurement fields.
deliverable: A sticky left-side summary column in the ledger table
Consolidation of row identity fields into a compact editable overview layout
Reduced ledger width compared with the previous multi-column identity layout
Verification that the current baseline remains green
constraints: Preserve existing inline-edit workflows for the consolidated fields
Keep the round bounded to ledger usability and horizontal context retention
Do not change backend data contracts or database schema
Keep supplier selection and purchase-link actions functional
acceptance_criteria: Users can still tell what row they are looking at when scrolling rightward in the ledger
The ledger has fewer standalone identity columns than before
Inline editing remains available for item name, serial number, department, handler, supplier, and purchase link
py -3 scripts/validate_project.py passes after the change
product_acceptance_criteria: The ledger feels more like a procurement workbench than a horizontally fragmented spreadsheet
The frozen left-side context continues to identify the row while downstream fields remain editable
manual_spot_checks: Open the ledger and scroll to the invoice and payment columns while confirming the left-side summary still identifies the row
Edit item name, supplier, and purchase link from the new summary block
Confirm the sticky summary and action columns still align correctly on selected or focused rows
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the sticky summary layout ships and baseline validation reruns successfully.
delivery_truth_contract: Describe only the ledger layout refactor, governed artifacts, and validation that actually ran.
non_goals: A card-view rewrite for the ledger
A full redesign of the ledger page outside the table itself
inferred_assumptions: The user's main pain is losing row context during horizontal scrolling, not the lack of raw data.
A frozen summary column is the fastest high-value improvement without destabilizing the rest of the ledger workflow.
